content: Autonomous curiosity-driven agents (Topic A) can leverage the 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agent memory architectures (Topic B) to build a 'curiosity memory buffer' that prioritizes retaining only the unexpected, learning-worthy experiences the agent encounters, rather than storing all interaction data. This structured, curiosity-filtered memory lets the agent actively request additional human or environmental data about high-uncertainty topics it self-identifies as interesting.
